title: Voynich Manuscript Preliminary Lexicon
methodology: Compiled from: (1) distributional word vectors (PPMI+SVD, 50d), (2) visual cross-referencing against illustrated content, (3) structural tokenization removing Latin alphabetic bias, (4) declension/paradigm analysis, (5) plant feature enrichment analysis, (6) label network cross-referencing across sections. Phonetic values are NOT assigned; all analysis is structural/distributional.

entries.meaning: Most common word; likely preposition, copula, or demonstrative ("this", "the", "is", "in")
entries.evidence: Highest frequency (829). Appears in all sections. Head of the da- paradigm with 7+ declined forms. Dominates herbal section (54%) suggesting it introduces/connects descriptions.

entries.meaning: Declined form of ol/or/ar/al paradigm
entries.evidence: Vector analogy ol:or::al:ar confirmed (cosine 0.72). Same paradigm as ol.

entries.meaning: Declined form of ol/or/ar/al paradigm
entries.evidence: Stars-heavy (48%). Vector analogy confirmed.

entries.meaning: Declined form of ol/or/ar/al paradigm
entries.evidence: Stars-heavy (63%). Strongly enriched on astronomical pages.

entries.meaning: Common word-final token; vowel or grammatical suffix
entries.evidence: Token Y has mean position 0.879 (strongly word-final). 11.9% of all tokens.

entries.meaning: Label appearing across Astro/Bio/Pharma/Zodiac; a key cross-reference term linking star diagrams to bath procedures to recipes
entries.evidence: The most connected label: f68r1 (star label), f71r (zodiac), f77r+f77v (bath tubes), f102v2 (pharma fragment). Four-way section bridge.

entries.meaning: Name of a brown multi-fingered root plant; visually confirmed across pharma pages. Cross-references zodiac (Scorpio?) and bath section.
entries.evidence: Visual verification: f88r and f99v both show brown multi-fingered root fragments. f73r labels nymph on Scorpio zodiac page. f84r labels bath flow. Complete pharma reference chain: zodiac timing в†’ bath procedure в†’ recipe ingredient.

entries.meaning: Declined form of otol; labels bath tube (f82v) and pharma fragments (f89r1, f89r2, f99r, f99v)
entries.evidence: Strongest Bioв†”Pharma bridge (5 folios). Appears as both container label and fragment label.

entries.meaning: Word-initial prefix; determiner, relative pronoun, or definite article
entries.evidence: Token QO has positional entropy 0.098 (virtually always word-initial). 97.6% q+o co-occurrence. Attaches to stems from all domains (qokedy=Bio, qoor=Pharma, qokedaiin=Stars). Forms the most productive prefix paradigm in the manuscript.

entries.meaning: Case/declension ending; short vs long vowel marks grammatical distinction (possibly nominative vs accusative, or definite vs indefinite)
entries.evidence: AII+N has positional entropy 0.217 (virtually always word-final). PMI 4.89. daiin/dain differ in collocation (Jaccard <0.2 in Lang A). 4.64x within-family vector similarity vs random baseline.

entries.meaning: Case/declension ending; Language B dominant suffix. Vowel length (ee vs e) marks grammatical distinction
entries.evidence: chedy/cheedy, shedy/sheedy, qokedy/qokeedy all show different collocations. -edy is the signature morpheme of Currier Language B.

entries.meaning: Case/declension ending; related to -edy system
entries.evidence: chey/cheey, shey/sheey show systematic paradigmatic behavior.

entries.meaning: Five-way case ending system on stems; the core declension paradigm
entries.evidence: Vector analogy ol:or::al:ar confirmed. Five endings attach to all major stems: ch-ol/-or, sh-ol/-or, ok-al/-ar/-am, ot-al/-ar. Suffix agreement ratio 1.70x (Lang A).

entries.meaning: Astronomical domain marker; possibly "star", "constellation", or "celestial body"
entries.evidence: Almost exclusively on Stars section pages (lkaiin: 45 astro / 5 other). Takes full declension endings (-aiin, -eey, -am, -al, -chdy). Too frequent for a proper noun; likely a common noun.

entries.meaning: NOT a phoneme. Structural left-bracket of the ch ligature and gallows cartouche. 99.8% followed by h/t/k/p/f.
entries.evidence: Only 29/12645 instances of c not followed by h/t/k/p/f. Functions as glyph connector.

entries.meaning: Single consonant unit (ligated cc). The most common consonant in the script.
entries.evidence: 10412 occurrences. Takes gallows modifiers: cth, ckh, cph, cfh. Position: mean 0.211 (word-initial tendency).

entries.meaning: Common function word; possibly "water", "of", or a case marker
entries.evidence: Second most common (534). Appears as prefix in water-page compounds (olkain, olshey, orol). Bio/bath section enriched (48%). Forms word-initial element in pool/water vocabulary.

entries.meaning: Common word-final element; possibly locative case marker ("in/at") or verbal suffix
entries.evidence: Appears in all sections. Sentence-initial clustering in Language B (chi2=75.3). As suffix: -dy/-edy/-eedy form the dominant Language B ending pattern.

entries.meaning: Short function word; possibly conjunction ("and") or sentence particle
entries.evidence: Appears in all 7 section types. High frequency (191). Herbal-enriched (67%).

entries.meaning: Pharma-enriched word (24%). Possibly names a container type, preparation method, or ingredient category
entries.evidence: Appears on jar-illustration pages. Related to cheor (also pharma-enriched). The -eol ending may mark pharma-specific vocabulary.

entries.meaning: Bio/bath section word (78% bio). Likely relates to bath/water procedure or nymph-associated concept
entries.evidence: Strongly enriched on pool/nymph pages. Nearest neighbors: qokain, chedy, ly, oly вЂ” all bio-section words.

entries.meaning: Herbal-exclusive word (88% herbal). Likely describes a plant property or processing step
entries.evidence: Appears on 44 herbal folios. Nearest neighbors: daiin, chor, tchol, qotchy, chol вЂ” all herbal vocabulary.

entries.meaning: Appears as second word in compound labels (okain am, otor am, sar am); possibly a unit marker, quantity, or temporal modifier
entries.evidence: Bridges Astro/Pharma/Zodiac. On zodiac pages appears with sign labels (otalchy tar am dy). On pharma pages modifies fragment labels.

entries.meaning: Strongly pharma-associated (52%). Likely names a specific preparation or container contents
entries.evidence: Nearest neighbor: qockheol (0.77). The -eol ending cluster is pharma-specific.

entries.meaning: Herbal exclusive (100%). Likely describes a plant property, processing step, or part name
entries.evidence: 16 occurrences, all herbal. Never in other sections.

entries.meaning: Bio/bath exclusive (100%). Labels or describes something specific to the bath/nymph illustrations
entries.evidence: 11 occurrences, all on Bio pages. Never in herbal or stars.

entries.meaning: Stars exclusive (100%). Likely a stellar/astronomical term
entries.evidence: 10 occurrences, all on Stars pages.

entries.meaning: Bath/water exclusive (100%). Contains ol- prefix. Likely water/pool-related
entries.evidence: 6 occurrences, all on pool/water pages.

entries.meaning: Word-initial element on labels; possibly Arabic definite article al- or a general determiner
entries.evidence: 67% of star labels and 90%+ of zodiac labels begin with o-. Arabic star names universally begin with al-. May be the same element as standalone "ol/or/al/ar" in declined form.

entries.meaning: Major content stem; takes all declension endings. Herbal and Bio sections. Possibly a general botanical term or common noun
entries.evidence: Second largest paradigm (585 endings, 5004 occurrences). CHol, CHor, CHey, CHedy are among the most common words.

entries.meaning: Major content stem parallel to ch-; same endings. Bio section enriched (60% for shedy)
entries.evidence: Third largest paradigm. SH- and CH- behave as parallel stems (vector analogy chol:chor::shol:shor confirmed). May be two related nouns or a grammatical alternation.

entries.meaning: Not standalone consonants but modifiers of adjacent characters. Create the cartouche construct c-gallows-h. May encode aspiration, emphasis, or phonological features.
entries.evidence: 63% of t preceded by o. 48% of p followed by c (в†’pch compound). True consonant inventory is ~8 base consonants with modifier variants.

entries.meaning: Enriched on brown-root plant pages; candidate for "brown" or "dark"
entries.evidence: Log-odds 3.63 for brown_root feature.

entries.meaning: Enriched on single-flower plant pages; candidate for "single" or "one"
entries.evidence: Log-odds 3.42 for single_flower feature. Also appears as zodiac/pharma label.

entries.meaning: Enriched on calyx-type flower pages; candidate for "cup-shaped" or descriptor of calyx structure
entries.evidence: Log-odds 5.08 for calyx feature.

entries.meaning: Enriched on seed-bearing plant pages; candidate for "seed" or "grain"
entries.evidence: Log-odds 3.46 for seeds feature.

entries.meaning: Enriched exclusively on blue-flower plant pages; short word, strong color term candidate for "blue"
entries.evidence: Log-odds 4.31 for blue_flowers feature. 3 occurrences, all on blue-flower pages.

entries.meaning: Enriched exclusively on flat-topped-root pages; candidate for "flat" or "truncated"
entries.evidence: Log-odds 5.29 for flat_topped_root. 4 occurrences, all on flat-root pages.

entries.meaning: Enriched on root-platform plant pages; candidate for "platform", "base", or "earth"
entries.evidence: Log-odds 6.34 for root_platform feature. 4 occurrences, all on platform-root pages.

entries.meaning: Enriched exclusively on blue-flower plant pages; candidate for color term "blue"
entries.evidence: Log-odds 4.73 for blue_flowers feature. Appears only on folios with blue-flowered plants.
